bash_commands.command: find /workspace/kafka--0753c489 -type f \( -name "*.java" -o -name "*.scala" \) | xargs grep -l "class.*Processor.*extends" | grep -E "(network|server)" | grep -v test | head -20

grep -n "^private\[kafka\] abstract class Acceptor\|^private\[kafka\] class Processor\|^class DataPlaneAcceptor\|^class NetworkProcessor" /workspace/kafka--0753c489/core/src/main/scala/kafka/network/SocketServer.scala | head -10
find /workspace/kafka--0753c489 -type f -name "*.java" | xargs grep -l "RequestChannel\|ConnectionQuotas\|ChannelBuilder" | grep -v test | head -20
find /workspace/kafka--0753c489 -type f -name "*.java" | xargs grep -l "class.*Processor\|class.*Acceptor" | grep -v test | grep -v Streams

find /workspace/kafka--0753c489 -type f -name "*.java" | xargs grep -l "Selector\|NetworkReceive\|NetworkSend\|KafkaChannel\|ChannelBuilder\|RequestChannel\|Socket.*Server\|Connection.*Quota" | grep -v test | sort | uniq

    {"repo": "apache/kafka", "path": "server/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/network/metrics/RequestMetrics.java"},
    {"repo": "apache/kafka", "path": "server-common/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/network/BrokerEndPoint.java"},
    {"repo": "apache/kafka", "path": "server-common/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/network/ConnectionDisconnectListener.java"},
    {"repo": "apache/kafka", "path": "server-common/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/network/EndpointReadyFutures.java"},
    {"repo": "apache/kafka", "path": "server-common/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/server/network/KafkaAuthorizerServerInfo.java"},
    {"repo": "apache/kafka", "path": "core/src/main/java/kafka/server/ClientRequestQuotaManager.java"},
    {"repo": "apache/kafka", "path": "core/src/main/java/kafka/server/NetworkUtils.java"},
    {"repo": "apache/kafka", "path": "core/src/main/java/kafka/server/ReplicaQuota.java"},
    {"repo": "apache/kafka", "path": "core/src/main/java/kafka/server/ReplicationQuotaManager.java"},
    {"repo": "apache/kafka", "path": "core/src/main/java/kafka/server/QuotaFactory.java"}
  ],
  "text": "Kafka's network layer in Java consists of multiple integrated components managed across several modules:\n\n**NIO-Based Selector and Channel Management** (org.apache.kafka.common.network):\nThe Selector class (clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/network/Selector.java) implements a non-blocking, multi-connection network I/O abstraction using Java NIO SelectionKeys. It works with KafkaChannel, which manages individual client connections and handles TLS/SASL authentication through pluggable TransportLayer implementations. The ChannelBuilder interface and its implementations (PlaintextChannelBuilder, SaslChannelBuilder, SslChannelBuilder) provide factory methods for creating secure channels.\n\n**Send/Receive Buffer Management** (org.apache.kafka.common.network):\nNetworkSend and NetworkReceive provide size-delimited protocol frame abstractions for transmitting requests and responses. ByteBufferSend offers direct ByteBuffer-based sending. The Send and Receive interfaces define the contract for network I/O primitives.\n\n**Transport Layer Abstraction** (org.apache.kafka.common.network):\nTransportLayer is an interface with implementations PlaintextTransportLayer and SslTransportLayer that handle the low-level reading/writing of bytes over SocketChannels, managing encryption and authentication protocols.\n\n**Request Channel Handoff and Server-Side Network Management** (org.apache.kafka.network and org.apache.kafka.server.network):\nWhile the core RequestChannel, Processor threads, and Acceptor thread are implemented in Scala (kafka.network.SocketServer and kafka.network.RequestChannel), they are supported by Java classes for metrics (RequestChannelMetrics, RequestMetrics), session management (Session.java), and configuration (SocketServerConfigs.java). The RequestConvertToJson utility provides JSON serialization for request/response logging.\n\n**Connection Management and Quotas** (org.apache.kafka.network, kafka.server):\nConnectionQuotaEntity, ConnectionThrottledException, and TooManyConnectionsException handle connection rate limiting. ClientRequestQuotaManager, ReplicationQuotaManager, and QuotaFactory implement request quota enforcement. ConnectionDisconnectListener provides hooks for connection lifecycle events.\n\n**Authentication and Security** (org.apache.kafka.common.network, org.apache.kafka.common.security.authenticator):\nAuthenticator interface and SaslClientAuthenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator provide SASL authentication support. ReauthenticationContext manages credential refresh during connection lifetime.\n\n**Configuration and Metadata** (org.apache.kafka.common.network, org.apache.kafka.server.network):\nListenerName, ClientInformation, ServerConnectionId, ChannelState, and ChannelMetadataRegistry manage network configuration, client metadata, and channel lifecycle information. BrokerEndPoint and KafkaAuthorizerServerInfo provide authorization integration points."
}

conversation_events.text:
# Kafka Network Layer Selector and Processor Architecture

## Your Task

Find all Java source files in apache/kafka that implement the network layer: the Selector (NIO-based), the Processor threads, the Acceptor thread, the RequestChannel handoff, and the NetworkReceive/NetworkSend byte buffer management.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the platform domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/kafka--0753c489, sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc, sg-evals/camel--1006f047.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/kafka--0753c489` (apache/kafka)
- `sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc` (apache/flink)
- `sg-evals/camel--1006f047` (apache/camel)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find all the Java source files that implement Kafka's network layer. Let me start by exploring the kafka repository structure.
Now let me create the comprehensive answer.json file with all the Java source files that implement Kafka's network layer:

Perfect! I've successfully created the comprehensive answer.json file at `/workspace/answer.json`. 

## Summary

I've identified **48 Java source files** in apache/kafka that implement the network layer:

### Key Components Found:

1. **NIO-Based Selector** (`Selector.java`) - Non-blocking multi-connection I/O using Java NIO
2. **Channel Management** - `KafkaChannel.java` and pluggable `ChannelBuilder` implementations (Plaintext, SASL, SSL)
3. **NetworkReceive/NetworkSend** - Protocol frame abstractions for size-delimited message transmission
4. **Transport Layer** - `TransportLayer` interface with TLS and plaintext implementations
5. **Authentication** - SASL client/server authenticators and reauthentication support
6. **Connection Management** - Connection quota enforcement, throttling, and lifecycle management
7. **Metrics** - `RequestChannelMetrics` and `RequestMetrics` for monitoring
8. **Server-side Support** - Configuration, session management, and request conversion utilities

### Important Note:
The **Processor threads**, **Acceptor thread**, and **RequestChannel** core classes are implemented in **Scala** (not Java):
- `kafka.network.SocketServer` - contains Processor and Acceptor classes
- `kafka.network.RequestChannel` - implements request/response channel handoff

These Scala components work with the Java network layer classes to handle the broker's network I/O operations. The answer file has been saved with detailed documentation of all Java files and their roles in the network architecture.

grep -n "^private\[kafka\] abstract class Acceptor\|^private\[kafka\] class Processor\|^class DataPlaneAcceptor\|^class NetworkProcessor" /workspace/kafka--0753c489/core/src/main/scala/kafka/network/SocketServer.scala | head -10

It works with KafkaChannel, which manages individual client connections and handles TLS/SASL authentication through pluggable TransportLayer implementations. ClientRequestQuotaManager, ReplicationQuotaManager, and QuotaFactory implement request quota enforcement. ConnectionDisconnectListener provides hooks for connection lifecycle events.\n\n**Authentication and Security** (org.apache.kafka.common.network, org.apache.kafka.common.security.authenticator):\nAuthenticator interface and SaslClientAuthenticator/SaslServerAuthenticator provide SASL authentication support. ReauthenticationContext manages credential refresh during connection lifetime.\n\n**Configuration and Metadata** (org.apache.kafka.common.network, org.apache.kafka.server.network):\nListenerName, ClientInformation, ServerConnectionId, ChannelState, and ChannelMetadataRegistry manage network configuration, client metadata, and channel lifecycle information. BrokerEndPoint and KafkaAuthorizerServerInfo provide authorization integration points."
}
